summary: Travel Advisor iteration 29 backend testing complete: pin/unpin, edit, delete, scan (AI Router fallback), and pinned-first list sort all pass. 11/11 tests green in 8.3s. Verified Perplexity 401 gracefully falls back to Claude — scan returns actual prices (one_way=650, round_trip=1100, hotel=120 for Tokyo test) and persists scan_result + last_scanned_at.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 6321 lines — well beyond the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into modules (travel, finance, career, security). Not blocking but degrades maintainability.
scan_trip endpoint has no rate-limit / cooldown. A user could hammer it and incur AI cost. Consider gating (e.g., min 60s between scans per trip).

TripIn body is fully-required for PUT /trips/{id} (destination_name & country mandatory) — frontend must always send full payload for partial edits. Consider a TripPatch model with optional fields to allow true PATCH semantics.
list_trips.to_list(200) hard-caps at 200 — fine for now but not paginated.
seed_data_creation: None — tests create and self-clean up TEST_-prefixed trips.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_29_travel_pin_scan.py covers all new travel endpoints (pin, scan, edit, delete, list sort) plus regression (insights, checklist, cost-estimate). Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Scan endpoint depends on ai_router Claude fallback path — the Perplexity key (236e7cf2-...) is currently invalid and reliably triggers the fallback path. Auto-cleanup removes any TEST_-prefixed trip before/after the module.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. AI Router's broadened `except Exception` in route_ai_task (ai_router.py:500) correctly catches Perplexity's 401 (raised by httpx.raise_for_status()) and cleanly routes to Claude via _call_claude_fallback. Verified platform_used='claude' with model_used='claude-sonnet-4-5' in scan response; JSON parsing of Claude output yields valid numeric prices.
